steps.title: Identify the dominant pattern
steps.content: The poem doesn't organize by categories or develop a single extended idea. Instead, it presents one vivid image after another—lizard, goose, cranes, twilight, sleeping marsh—each adding another visual snapshot of the same location.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content: The series of successive images paints the setting through visual snapshots rather than through categories or extended metaphors. The other choices misrepresent the structure: A wrongly separates animals from plants into distinct groups; C falsely claims an extended emotional metaphor; D invents a person who never appears. That matches choice B — B is correct.
